items.title: MP laments 'horrifying' amount lost to pokies by Tasmanians

children.content: Tasmanians lost $208.27 million to poker machines in 2025-26, or around $360 per person — a 6.9 per cent increase on last financial year.

gallery.altText: Gambling addiction advocates want new laws applied to all poker machine operators.
A distorted picture of a woman playing the pokies.

thumbnail.altText: Gambling addiction advocates want new laws applied to all poker machine operators.
A distorted picture of a woman playing the pokies.

items.title: How clogging and crafting saved a rural Tasmanian town hall from closure

children.content: Every Sunday, a group of men and women get together for some American clogging, home-baked afternoon tea and community connection.

image.altText: a group of women dance in tap shoes in an old wooden-walled building

items.title: Going after the media a tried and true tactic of diversion

children.content: A minister of the Crown's scolding of a journalist asking a legitimate question about firearm ownership, and the subsequent pile-on allowed to run on social media, should ring alarm bells for anyone concerned with democracy.

image.altText: A bearded man speaking at press conference.

avatar.alt: A woman with glasses stands in a TV studio.

items.title: Concerns smelter closure exposes Australia to geopolitical risk

children.content: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

articles.title: Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter to close immediately

children.content: Australia's only manganese smelter, in northern Tasmania, will close immediately after administrators failed to find a buyer for the former GFG Alliance asset.

image.altText: smelter factory external shots

children.content: Australia's only manganese smelter is ceasing operations, leaving more than 200 employees without work in northern Tasmania.

image.altText: A man wearing a bright orange shirt standing outside

gallery.altText: A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.

thumbnail.altText: A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.

items.title: Sam Neill's advice for when the world seems too dark

children.content: The world has been remembering Sam Neill's incredible film and television career in the wake of the actor's death, but his off-screen antics left a robust impact.

gallery.altText: Sam Neill and his young Hunt For The Wilderpeople co-star Julian Dennison reunite at Tropfest Sydney 2017.

thumbnail.altText: Sam Neill and his young Hunt For The Wilderpeople co-star Julian Dennison reunite at Tropfest Sydney 2017.

items.title: Australian stuns The Open with equal lowest round in major golf history

children.content: Australia's Lucas Herbert takes a shock lead at The Open after posting a stunning round where he birdied six of his opening nine holes.

image.altText: A golfer in a maroon shirt lifts his white cap to acknowledge crowd on fairway

items.title: 'Bring back public flogging': Inquiry hears of vicious online trolling

children.content: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

image.altText: Racism Hearing Inquiry committee

items.title: Sydney suburbs which recorded rent price drops, as 'real test' looms

children.content: The June quarterly rent report from Domain paints a mixed picture of Sydney's market, including both major surges and significant drops for rent prices in certain suburbs.

image.altText: For lease for rent signs in Sydney in front of apartments and houses.

items.title: Antisemitism inquiry turns uncomfortable lens on university protests

children.content: Universities have apologised to Jewish staff and students who have spoken of their distress at being targeted by protest movement.

image.altText: A group of protesters cheering and waving flags.

avatar.alt: Reporter James Oaten wearing a navy button-up shirt.

items.title: The quote that shows the divide within Australia's Commonwealth Games team

children.content: While a Commonwealth Games gold rush is always expected for Australia's swimmers, the emerging generation of track and field athletes see Glasgow as a chance to make their mark on a global stage.

gallery.altText: Oliver Hoare shouts to celebrate winning the 1,500m at the 2022 Birmingham Commonwealth Games.

thumbnail.altText: Oliver Hoare shouts to celebrate winning the 1,500m at the 2022 Birmingham Commonwealth Games.

items.title: Lao authorities charge distillery owners over fatal methanol poisoning

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: 'Palestine will disappear before our eyes': Calls for Israel to stop settlements

children.content: The two former world leaders are demanding the international community heap more pressure on Israel to change course.

image.altText: A woman shaded under trees stares towards the camera.

items.title: From Book Doof to bedazzling, social reading is all the rage

children.content: People find community with other bookworms as social reading takes off with everything from literature raves to book bedazzling.

items.title: Grey's Anatomy mixed with Bump and Scrubs: Welcome to The F Ward

children.content: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

image.altText: Ioane Sa'ula, Samoan Australian, mid-20s, in orange scrubs, sits in a hospital waiting area, leaning forward, hands clasped.

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Sam Neill's Marvel cameo, 70s punk rock and traffic cones

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Burnham to become next UK PM after Australian candidate gets one vote

children.content: Andy Burnham will be Britain's seventh prime minister in 10 years next week after winning a Labour leadership contest in which his only challenger, an Australian, received just one vote.

items.title: More than 20 SA Department for Child Protection employees stood down

children.content: At the end of February, South Australia's Department for Child Protection had 12 open investigations into staff as a result of a care concern, Freedom of Information documents show.

image.altText: Empty swings in a park with a pram parked in the background.

items.title: The LNP's new crime statistics could be comparing 'apples and oranges'

children.content: Queensland's premier says new statistics show crime is falling across the state, but his data sources are inconsistent.

image.altText: A graphic of Queensland Premier David Crisafulli superimposed with the QPS logo.

items.title: Brenda Fricker, Oscar-winning Irish actress, dies aged 81

children.content: Brenda Fricker became the first Irish actress to win an Oscar for the 1989 film My Left Foot and also played the kindly Pigeon Lady in Home Alone 2.

items.title: Drug-resistant hookworm found in growing number of retired greyhounds

children.content: Greyhound adoption groups and specialist vets say they're seeing a growing number of retired racing dogs infected with a deadly treatment-resistant parasite, raising concerns about animal welfare, adoption costs and the spread of the disease beyond the racing industry.

image.altText: A happy greyhound sitting on a bed in Ormeau, Queensland.

items.title: 'Divided response': Presbyterian Church bars women from senior roles

children.content: A vote excluding women from elder positions in the Presbyterian Church in NSW and the ACT is disappointing but not surprising, a long-standing parishioner says.

image.altText: Presbyterian Church parishioner Sonia Thew

items.title: Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies aged 89

children.content: Sir Don Bradman once described Sobers as "the greatest all-round cricketer I ever saw".

image.altText: Garry Sobers 2

items.title: City investors buy shares in oysters, cows and bumper crops

children.content: An Australian investment platform is helping farmers raise capital by connecting them with everyday investors who can buy shares in crops and livestock while sharing the risks and rewards of farming.

image.altText: Image of oysters, cattle and wheat crops.

items.title: 'Unethical': Minister admits big tech firms use Aussie content without artists' permission

children.content: Australian artists have already had their music taken to train overseas AI models without payment or consent.

items.title: No guarantee outages won't happen again, Telstra CEO says

children.content: The national Telstra outage last week was triggered by maintenance work that caused the system's clocks to go back two decades, the telco says.

image.altText: The orange and white telstra sign is in focus in the background as people walk past in a blur.

items.title: Eight killed, dozens missing as landslide buries people in China

children.content: A landslide in south-western China's Chongqing municipality has buried people in rubble and destroyed a roadway and a number of buildings, according to state media.

image.altText: Large boulders lie across a roadway amid the wreckage of buildings by a mountainside.

items.title: Trump's speech a desperate move — and 'ominous sign' ahead of midterms

children.content: Using a prime-time address to the nation to repeat long-debunked claims about the 2020 election defeat is a desperate and unusual move. Analysts say it's an "ominous sign" of how Donald Trump may handle the upcoming midterms.

articles.title: Trump uses address to lay out litany of election integrity claims

children.content: US President Donald Trump uses a presidential speech to accuse China of improperly trying to influence the 2020 election and stop him from winning the presidency.

image.altText: Two people in a voting booth. Their faces are obscured but their legs are visible.

gallery.altText: Two people in a voting booth. Their faces are obscured but their legs are visible.

thumbnail.altText: Two people in a voting booth. Their faces are obscured but their legs are visible.

avatar.alt: Lauren Day pictured in front of an image of the US Capitol.

items.title: Ukraine says it destroyed Russian bomber at Engels airbase

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the military plane was used by Russia for missile strikes on Ukraine.

image.altText: Across a brown air field, you see a large military plane with four propellers rotating.

items.title: Moira Deeming ousted as Liberal candidate after apology stand-off

children.content: Moira Deeming's political career as a Liberal is over after the party's Victorian state executive voted to revoke the MP's candidacy for the November state election.

items.title: Star Swan takes MOTY contender as Crows notch vital win

children.content: The Adelaide Crows beat the Swans in a thriller in the wet at the SCG to jump into the top four.

items.title: Melbourne's injury crisis deepens as Warbrick's season ends in loss to Roosters

children.content: Will Warbrick's Melbourne Storm career appears to be over, with the departing winger rupturing his Achilles in his side's 14-6 loss to the Sydney Roosters.

items.title: Decade-long friendship between Roy Orbison's hit maker and Australian singer

children.content: Every Monday morning for the last 14 years, Australian musician Damien Leith has been meeting with the late Joe Melson, the Nashville songwriter responsible for some of Roy Orbison's biggest hits.

image.altText: Damien plays a guitar while sitting in a back car seat next to Joe Melson, who wears a black coat.

items.title: Man jailed after stepfather's body 'left to rot' for 14 months

children.content: Nikola Golem lied to his "mentally unwell" mother about her husband's death for more than a year, and let her sleep beside the decomposing body, Brisbane's Supreme Court heard.
